name: Horcajo de Montes from Alcoba — circular via Navalpino
name_original: Horcajo de Montes desde Alcoba — circular por Navalpino
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcoba offers routes through the natural landscapes of the region, including areas near the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros. The terrain features varied elevations, with routes often incorporating climbs and descents. Cyclists can expect to encounter reservoirs and rural paths, providing a mix of natural and semi-natural environments. Best touring cycling routes around Alcoba The most popular touring cycling route is Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — circular by Horcajo de los Montes, a difficult 22.8 miles (36.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route explores the edges of the national park, offering a challenging ride with significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular desde Alcoba, a moderate 44.3 miles (71.3 km) path. This route circles the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, providing views of the water and surrounding natural areas. Local touring cyclists also love the Horcajo de Montes from Alcoba — circular via Navalpino, a 35.7 miles (57.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past the village of Navalpino, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcoba is defined by routes through national park areas, around reservoirs, and across varied rural terrain. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels, catering to cyclists seeking longer, more challenging rides. The routes in Alcoba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcoba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Alcoba?
A: Touring cycling routes around Alcoba feature varied elevations, often incorporating climbs and descents. You'll encounter natural landscapes, including areas near the Parque Nacional de Cabañeros, reservoirs, and rural paths, offering a mix of natural and semi-natural environments.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcoba?
A: The routes around Alcoba tend to be moderate to difficult, catering to cyclists seeking longer, more challenging rides. Currently, there are no routes specifically classified as easy for touring cycling in this area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available in Alcoba?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, you can try the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058383946/parque-nacional-de-cabaneros-circular-by-horcajo-de-los-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Cabañeros — circular by Horcajo de los Montes&lt;/a&gt;, or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058357821/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-circular-desde-alcoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular desde Alcoba&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the Torre de Abraham Reservoir.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can discover several natural highlights. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5634889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera de Horcajo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6325378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Grandfather Cork Oak&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4319882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in Alcoba?
A: Yes, Alcoba offers several longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1186633221/return-to-cabaneros-national-park-via-the-torre-de-abraham-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Cabañeros National Park via the Torre de Abraham Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.3-mile (76.2 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057546744/horcajo-de-montes-from-alcoba-circular-via-navalpino" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Montes from Alcoba — circular via Navalpino&lt;/a&gt; covers 35.7 miles (57.5 km).

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alcoba?
A: Given the region's climate, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the most pleasant times for touring cycling in Alcoba. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, especially in the national park areas.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alcoba?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcoba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging terrain and the natural beauty of the Cabañeros National Park area.

Q: Is it possible to find cafes or pubs near the touring cycling trails in Alcoba?
A: Alcoba and its surrounding villages are rural. While you might find local establishments in towns like Horcajo de los Montes or Navalpino, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes that traverse more remote sections of the national park.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by reservoirs or offer opportunities for wild swimming?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058357821/embalse-de-torre-de-abraham-circular-desde-alcoba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Torre de Abraham — circular desde Alcoba&lt;/a&gt; route circles the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, providing scenic views of the water. While the routes pass by reservoirs, always check local regulations regarding swimming, as wild swimming may not be permitted or safe in all areas.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Alcoba?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for elevation changes, layered clothing is recommended. Breathable, moisture-wicking fabrics are ideal. Depending on the season, you might need a windbreaker or rain jacket. Always wear a helmet and consider padded cycling shorts for comfort on longer rides.

Q: Are there any hidden gem touring cycling trails in Alcoba that are less frequented?
A: While all routes offer a sense of natural immersion, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40008171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de los Montes – Bohonal loop from Horcajo de los Montes&lt;/a&gt; provides a challenging ride through less-traveled areas, offering a more secluded experience within the broader Cabañeros region.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in the Alcoba region?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes currently available in the Alcoba region, ranging from moderate to difficult in intensity.
tips: Horcajo has a unique charm: its municipal area is integrated into the Cabañeros National Park. 1 km from the town of Horcajo, is the largest visitor center in the Park, essential to plan a visit. There, a main exhibition shows the different ecosystems of Cabañeros throughout the four seasons of the year. In addition, a quintería in which the culture and traditions of the region are made known. It is completed with projection rooms, a library, a picnic area, parking lots, etc.

Alcoba is located next to the Cabañeros National Park, it is one of the most valuable protected corners of the Montes de Toledo, a refuge for large birds of prey, black storks, eagles, vultures and many other endangered species.

Its landscape is made up of extensive pastures or meadows with rich seasonal pastures, the mountains and massifs provide shelter to a wide variety of birds and mammals.

Its magnificent natural environment invites you to carry out all types of activities related to nature.

The most significant element of its urban center is the Church of Our Lady of Consolation from the 17th century.
